task.prompt: Write a function `unique(lst)` that removes duplicate elements from a list and returns a list of unique elements.

gold_spec.goal: Remove duplicate elements, preserving the order of first occurrence.
postconditions: every element in result appears exactly once
every element in result was in lst
the relative order of elements in result matches their first-occurrence order in lst
every element in lst appears in result
edge_cases: no duplicates — returns the list unchanged
all elements identical — returns single-element list
acceptance_criteria: returns unique elements in first-occurrence order

known_weakness.description: The task says 'removes duplicates' but does not specify order preservation. A naive spec might allow returning elements in any order (e.g. sorted, or set-order). The test cases enforce first-occurrence order, which the spec must capture. Using `set(lst)` would fail because sets don't preserve insertion order reliably across Python versions.
